THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY
NASA
The Goals
• Develop revolutionary new to technologies including:
Lower emissions (e.g. 70-80% Nox reduction
Confine landing/takeoff noise foot print to airport boundary
Increase efficiency (~15-25% less fuel consumption) by 
advanced lightweight materials, reduce drag
• Increase lift (double lift coefficient to ~6) to open many more 
airports, rapid climb-out/descent for reduced noise, smaller 
wing for lower drag

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

Low speed research compressor 
4 major configuration tests  1998 – 2007
GOAL:  - demonstrate advanced blading concepts
TECH56 high speed compressor
4 major compressor rig tests   2000 – 2007
GOAL:  - demonstrate advanced concepts at high speed
- demonstrate efficiency and operability
TECH56 compressor test
Improved modeling led to next generation rotor tip features
- 10% fewer blades 
- 15% improvement in stall margin
- 1.5% improvement in efficiency
THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

• Low solidity, highly loaded  design
• Materials
– Blades: advanced turbine airfoil material (ATAMS)
– Nozzles & shrouds:  Ceramic Matrix Composite (CMC) 
• Efficiency
• Weight
• Durability
• 3 builds tested
– Turbine vane frame
– New aero technologies
– Reduced shock blade
Additional tests:
- HPT vane cascade
- Turbine vane frame cascade

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY
Study Engines
•
A 2000 State Of the Art (SOA) & an advanced turbofan (with the same thermo properties as the 
VCE) were created to assess VCE performance
•
Engines were sized to produce takeoff thrust required by each mission
•
Fan PR was varied until each had the same overall mass flow
•
High specific thrust design points resulted in low bypass configurations
•
Following parameters were common across all vision mission studies 
2000 SOA Turbofan
Advanced Turbofan

a  Both the advanced turbofan and variable cycle utilize cooled cooling air
b  Variable cycle modulates cooling at cruise power

THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY
Performance Analysis
•
All three study engines were evaluated in the same notional airframe; engine weights vary with 
technology level & addition of variable features
•
VCE accomplishes stated mission with 31% less fuel than yr 2000 cycle
–
Or it can carry 99% more cargo over the same range
–
Or it can carry the stated cargo 63% further
•
These translate into operational benefits including reductions in air refueling, sorties generated, 
deployed aircraft/crew & increase standoff
Tactical mobility mission performance
